AA:SS Orientation

This page is under construction.

This page will eventually help current students understand how to approach Advanced Astronomy: Solar System successfully.

What will appear here

  • How to think about AA:SS as a physics-based astronomy course rather than a descriptive survey
  • How to use notes, textbook reading, and packet work effectively
  • How geometry, algebra, and physical reasoning show up in the course
  • How to approach derivations, calculations, and group work
  • Common difficulties in topics such as celestial geometry, Kepler, the Sun, and Jupiter

Related pages

  • AA:SS overview
  • AA:X shared foundation
  • AA:SS packet hub (to be added)
